name: gov-banner
title: Gov Banner
description: A local-government trust banner with a native disclosure that explains who operates the site and what a secure connection does. It does not assume a .gov domain. Explainer content stays in the DOM (hidden attribute) so screen readers and find-in-page behave predictably.
useWhen: The first informational region on a public-facing local government website, after the skip link
Establishing trust before users share personal information or start a service flow
Sites serving multilingual audiences (every string is a prop, so the banner can be fully localized per page)
avoidWhen: Non-government products or internal tools where the official-website claim would be untrue
Announcing emergencies or service disruptions (use a site alert instead)
More than once per page (one banner, above the header, only)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the "How to verify this site" disclosure button
Enter or Space toggles the explainer content
aria-expanded reflects the current state and aria-controls references the explainer panel
Collapsed content is removed from the tab order via the hidden attribute (no focusable elements are hidden while reachable)

usage.example: } />
